hazard.summary: YFinance rate-limit errors propagated through the agent tool path and stalled analysis because rate-limit signals were not consistently preserved into vendor fallback logic.
signals: YFRateLimitError: Too Many Requests. Rate limited. Try after a while.
route_to_vendor fallback originally caught AlphaVantageRateLimitError but not YFRateLimitError
LangGraph tool execution surfaced the rate-limit exception during tools_market task
fix added retry/backoff, TTL caching, and vendor fallback handling for YFRateLimitError

constraints: Rate-limit exceptions from vendor adapters must propagate to the routing layer before generic exception handling
Vendor fallback logic must recognize equivalent rate-limit signals across providers
Repeated provider calls under rate limit must be bounded by retry/backoff and cache policy
Agent workflows must treat provider rate limits as recoverable routing events, not unhandled tool crashes
verification: Simulate yfinance raising YFRateLimitError from a market data call
Verify retry/backoff is applied before failure is surfaced
Verify cached yfinance responses avoid repeated provider calls within TTL
Verify route_to_vendor catches YFRateLimitError and attempts configured fallback vendors
Verify generic exception handlers do not swallow rate-limit exceptions before fallback logic can act
notes: This is not merely a missing retry bug. The deeper failure is that a provider-specific rate-limit signal did not consistently reach the routing/fallback layer. The fix combines backoff, TTL caching, and explicit propagation of YFRateLimitError into vendor fallback handling.

constraints: vendor rate-limit errors must reach fallback logic
cache policy must reduce repeated calls during provider pressure
rate-limit exceptions must not be swallowed by broad handlers
anti_patterns: provider-specific rate-limit exception not included in fallback handling
uncached repeated calls during rate limit
agent workflow crashes instead of routing around provider pressure
